title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum will play against the Washington Wizards Saturday after managing his right Achilles with a day off.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are listed as probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out due to a finger fracture.
Tatum is playing on a minutes restriction, averaging 27 minutes in his three games since returning from injury.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' featuring Alan Ritchson is now streaming on Netflix, focusing on a soldier facing PTSD.
'Zootopia 2' arrives on Disney+ with Nick and Judy tackling a mystery in their massive animal city.
HBO Max's 'Rooster' stars Steve Carell as a beach-read author looking for a fresh start on a college campus.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots acquired offensive guard Alijah Vera-Tucker, formerly of the Jets, to strengthen their offensive line.
Vera-Tucker has played 43 games but missed last season with injuries, including a triceps tear.
Morgan Moses influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join Patriots due to their strong culture and coaching.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Manager Alex Cora named Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox.
Story, 33, played 157 games last year, leading team in home runs, RBI, and total hits.
Crochet led MLB in strikeouts and is compared to elite pitchers, becoming a team leader with consistent performances.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC debuts in the NWSL against defending champions Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims for an attacking style with a potential 4-4-2 formation, despite roster newness.
The match features freshly installed grass at Gillette Stadium, expected to complement Legacy FC's possession game.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider stopping the twice-yearly clock change to adopt a permanent time system.
Senate President Karen E. Spilka supports bill S.2157, which proposes permanent Atlantic Standard Time for Massachusetts.
Research suggests time changes increase heart attack risk and disrupt routines; the Senate reviews the proposal.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announced the 2026 Pride celebration will take place on Saturday, June 6.
The parade will start at 11 a.m. in Copley Square, ending with a festival at Boston Common.
More information and volunteer opportunities for the event are available on the Boston Pride For The People website.

title: Feds release teen detained by ICE in Marlborough
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A 14-year-old Brazilian girl detained by ICE in Marlborough was released and returned to Massachusetts by federal officials.
Federal judge ordered her release amid claims she was used to locate her father; DHS says she was 'rescued' from gang members.
The girl is set to appear in immigration court; her lawyer aims to secure her legal residency in the U.S.

title: With ‘top guys’ struggling, a lineup reshuffle might be needed for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are facing a scoring slump post-Olympic break, jeopardizing their playoff hopes with inconsistent performances.
David Pastrnak, despite seven points in eight games, struggles at 5-on-5 play, prompting potential lineup changes.
Coach Marco Sturm considers changing lineups, possibly splitting Pastrnak and Elias Lindholm, to boost the Bruins' offense.

title: Swiss student came to Boston to study rehab. Now he’s running for trauma survivors.
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Alexis Cogne, a Swiss researcher at Harvard, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Stepping Strong Center.
Cogne aims to raise $10,000 for trauma research and rehabilitation programs at Brigham and Women's Hospital.
His Boston year focuses on neurorehabilitation, inspired by Switzerland's recent devastating fire underscoring trauma support needs.
